unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this first unit, your child learns the words for things in the classroom, some colours, and how to say their own name and age. They meet the idea that school is a happy, welcoming place. The goal is warm, confident first steps in English — not perfect spelling.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

visual.alt: Child-friendly illustration for Welcome to School

readings.title: Amal's First Day
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
Today was Amal's first day at school. She wore her new dress and held her mother's hand.

“Hello!” said a kind lady at the door. “I am your teacher. What is your name?”

“My name is Amal,” she said softly. “I am six years old.”

“Welcome, Amal!” said the teacher. “Come and see our classroom.”

Inside, Amal saw many things. There were tables and chairs. There were books and crayons. There was a big clock on the wall.

“Look!” said Amal. “A red book! And a blue pencil!”

A boy smiled at her. “Hello, my name is Adam,” he said. “Do you want to be my friend?”

“Yes!” said Amal. She was happy now.

The teacher said, “Let us sing!” All the children sang the alphabet song: “A, B, C, D…” Amal sang too.

Then the children did a treasure hunt. “Find something green!” said the teacher. Amal found a green crayon. “I can see a green crayon!” she said.

At the end of the day, Amal drew a picture of her school. She drew the tables, the chairs, and her new friend Adam.

“I like my school,” said Amal.

On the way home, her mother asked, “What did you do today?”

Selma smiled. “I learned new words. I made a friend. And I can sing the alphabet song!”

readings.title: Talk about Welcome to School
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. This is a ___. It is ___. My name is ___. I am ___ years old. I can ___. I like ___. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: Welcome to School rhyme
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
The “Hello, School!” poem — say it together
Hello, school!  /  Tables and chairs,  /  A list of rules,  /  Books and crayons.  /  Hello, school!
Point to each thing (a table, a chair, a book) as you say its name. Say it three times — a little louder each time!

grammar.title: Name a thing in your classroom
grammar.explanation: Hold up one classroom object and name it. Use 'This is a' when the thing is close to you.
grammar.commonMistake: The little word 'a' is easily dropped: 'This is pencil.' Say it together slowly so 'a' is heard.
grammar.memoryTip: Touch the object as you say 'This', then name it.
grammar.practice: Name three things on your desk: This is a pen. This is a rubber. This is a book.

grammar.title: Describe it with one word
grammar.explanation: After naming something, tell us about it. Say 'It is' and add a colour or size word.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children say 'It big.' The word 'is' must stay in the middle: 'It is big.'
grammar.memoryTip: Point at the thing and say 'It is' first, then your describing word.
grammar.practice: Describe three school things: It is big. It is red. It is new.

grammar.title: Introduce yourself by name
grammar.explanation: Stand tall, smile, and say your own name. Use this sentence whenever you meet someone new.
grammar.commonMistake: Children sometimes answer with only their name. Warmly encourage the full sentence: 'My name is...'.
grammar.memoryTip: Put your hand on your chest as you say 'My name is'.
grammar.practice: Practise three times with a friend: My name is Musa. My name is Aisha. My name is Yusuf.

grammar.title: Say how old you are
grammar.explanation: Hold up your fingers for your age, then say the whole sentence: I am six years old.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children say 'I have six years.' In English we say 'I am six years old.'
grammar.memoryTip: Show your age on your fingers while you speak: fingers first, words after.
grammar.practice: Say your own age, then a friend's: I am six years old. I am five years old. I am seven years old.

grammar.title: Share a school skill you have
grammar.explanation: Tell your class one thing you do well at school. Begin with 'I can' and add the action.
grammar.commonMistake: 'I can to write' is a common slip. Drop the little word 'to': 'I can write.'
grammar.memoryTip: Count your skills on your fingers as you say each 'I can' sentence.
grammar.practice: Say three school skills: I can write. I can count. I can tidy up.

grammar.title: Say what you like at school
grammar.explanation: Share something you enjoy. Say 'I like' and then add the thing or the activity.
grammar.commonMistake: Children may stop after 'I like' with nothing after it. Remind them the sentence needs a thing at the end.
grammar.memoryTip: Give a thumbs up every time you say 'I like'.
grammar.practice: Say three things you enjoy: I like books. I like painting. I like break time.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: This is a ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern This is a ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: It is ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern It is ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern My name is ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I am ___ years old. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: I can ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn

speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I like ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw one idea about school, colours and confident introductions. Tell an adult what it shows.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace two useful words, saying each sound or word as you trace.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Match three words to pictures, then copy one word.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your drawing or picture.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose a picture and complete one short sentence using: I like ___.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

activities.title: Activity 1
activities.instructionsAndItems: 1.  Colour the classroom. Find and colour: the red book, the blue pencil, the green chair.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 2
activities.instructionsAndItems: 2.  Draw YOU at school. Then say: “My name is ______. I am ______ years old.”

activities.title: Activity 4

activities.title: Activity 5

assignments.title: My Welcome to School show-and-tell page
assignments.instructions: Submit one drawing or made object, two labels or traced words, one recorded spoken pattern and an adult-supported reflection.
assignments.submissionType: Drawing or object + labels + short audio/video + reflection

liveSessions.title: Celebration and checkpoint
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete the previous short practice with an adult.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Welcome to School to an adult.

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name common classroom objects (table, chair, book, pencil…).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can name and point to colours (red, blue, green, yellow…).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say “My name is ___. I am ___ years old. I like ___.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say the names of some school actions (read, write, draw, sing).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can join in the alphabet song and a school poem.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can use short spoken and early-written English about school, colours and confident introductions.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself
